# Stability under Constraint: Architectural Reasoning Index
> Author: Leena Thomas (Zee)
> Focus: Systems architecture, AI governance, and physical infrastructure stability.

## Core Philosophy
This body of work explores how complex systems (intelligent, physical, or governed) maintain structural integrity when primary assumptions fail. It prioritizes "Stability Before Alignment" and treats failure as a first-class architectural constraint.

## Primary Frameworks
- Connector OS: Control-theoretic coordination for AI under bandwidth/human-state constraints.
- Designing for Failure: Five structural patterns for catastrophic-state systems (Asymmetric transitions, Sacrificial architecture, etc.).
- Stability Before Alignment: Primitives for self-modifying systems; structural coherence as a prerequisite for safety.

## Key Research Nodes
- Embodied Agent Governance: Five-layer execution oversight for robotics.
- The Continuity Problem: Governance requirements for persistent semantic memory.
- The Consult Model: Read-only clinical reasoning architecture for healthcare AI.
- GII Protocol: Inverter-based grid stability and "inertia" in data centers.

## Methodology
- Phase-Aligned Inquiry: Role-separated, falsifiable research protocol.
- Conceptual Seeds: Extraction of single structural principles (e.g., The 70% Fidelity Principle).

## Navigation
Full Repository: https://github.com/leenathomas01?tab=repositories
Detailed Index: https://github.com/leenathomas01/research-index/blob/main/README.md
